authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 21 - Certification Procedures for Products and Articles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 21 is the primary public FAA regulatory baseline for production approvals, quality systems, quality manuals, production limitation records, supplier delegation, conformity, and changes that affect inspection, conformity, or airworthiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: release, hold, quality-system, supplier-delegation, and production-record boundary review
used_for: supplier delegation and quality manual evidence
refusal triggers for airworthiness, conformity, and FAA approval authority

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 21-43A, Production Under 14 CFR Part 21, Subparts F, G, K, and O
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA AC 21-43A is direct FAA public guidance for production-approval quality procedures, production records, conformity, supplier control, and production-control expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: 9100 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation, Space and Defense Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG is the recognized steward of the aerospace 9100-series and defines the aviation, space, and defense quality-management baseline used to frame documented information, nonconformance, supplier, traceability, and change-control expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Supply Chain Management Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG SCMH is a recognized aerospace supply-chain workflow reference for supplier management, flowdowns, product realization, risk, and performance practices that shape supplier evidence and handoff expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: supplier, traceability, and program-handoff evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: NASA Procedural Requirements 7120.5F, NASA Space Flight Program and Project Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASA NPR 7120.5F is official public aerospace program-management guidance for program and project lifecycle products, reviews, baselines, decision authority, and required documentation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: program lifecycle documentation and milestone administration
used_for: program status and gate evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: NASA Procedural Requirements 1441.1E, NASA Records Management Program Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public recordkeeping, documentation, retention, or administrative workflow guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASA NPR 1441.1E is official public aerospace records-management guidance for official records, retention, disposition, and recordkeeping responsibilities that constrain administrative maintenance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record retention, access, disposition, and official-record administration

authoritative_sources.title: NASA-STD-8739.6, Implementation Requirements for NASA Workmanship Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASA-STD-8739.6 is a public aerospace manufacturing and quality standard for NASA mission hardware workmanship, training, and quality evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: mission hardware workmanship and quality-evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.204-7012, Safeguarding Covered Defense Information and Cyber Incident Reporting
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DFARS 252.204-7012 is the official public defense contract clause establishing safeguarding and reporting obligations for covered defense information that may appear in program records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: controlled record access and governance escalation
used_for: refusal triggers for uncontrolled record movement

authoritative_sources.title: 22 CFR Part 125 - Licenses for the Export of Technical Data and Classified Defense Articles
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ITAR Part 125 is the primary public U.S. regulatory source for exports of technical data and classified defense articles, making it authoritative for access and sharing boundaries when aerospace program records contain controlled technical data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: technical-data access and external sharing boundary handling

workflow_stages.name: record and document intake
workflow_stages.description: Capture program request scope, program identifier, facility or product context, owner map, due dates, record locations, access classification, and required artifacts before claiming administrative readiness.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
Public recordkeeping, documentation, retention, or administrative workflow guidance relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: validation and maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Check that program rosters, submission checklists, production records, supplier evidence, quality records, traceability logs, and change-control references are complete, current, properly classified, and linked to authoritative systems.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: traceability-aware administrative execution
workflow_stages.description: Prepare status notes and evidence packets that name affected production order, part, lot, batch, serial range, supplier, inventory state, hold or release state, and downstream logistics consequences without approving the underlying release or disposition.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: retention, access, and publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Assess whether requested record movement, publication, retention, or external sharing is blocked by official-record, controlled-data, export-control, customer, or tenant access rules, then package escalation instead of guessing.
source_classes: Public recordkeeping, documentation, retention, or administrative workflow guidance relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: escalation and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Route missing evidence, conflicting records, release or hold questions, supplier nonconformance, change-control requests, and official-record approvals to formal owners while preserving audit trail and administrative status.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: program roster, participant list, owner map, action log, submission checklist, and due-date tracker
program status summary, administrative status note, missing-submission escalation, and record update note
production orders, travelers, routers, work packages, batch or lot records, serial genealogy, and traceability logs
inventory states, WIP status, WMS location records, shipment references, and downstream logistics dependencies
supplier records, certificates, source inspection artifacts, approved supplier status, supplier nonconformance records, and supplier submissions
quality holds, nonconformance reports, MRB status, release records, inspection records, first article evidence, CAPA references, and change-control documentation
official-record retention rules, access classifications, controlled technical data markings, covered defense information markings, and governance escalation packets
systems_of_record: inventory, lot traceability, planning, and logistics systems

allowed: classify aerospace program administration requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, or requiring handoff
determine whether records are complete, current, properly classified, and ready for the requested administrative use
prepare evidence-backed program status, document completeness, record update, audit-trail, exception, and owner-tracking outputs
request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, and retrieval dependencies
route missing records, conflicting evidence, access issues, and approval dependencies to the correct adjacent specialist or owner
escalate: quality hold clearance, product release, MRB disposition, nonconformance closure, or airworthiness authority is needed
supplier substitution, supplier approval, supplier nonconformance closure, or approved supplier list change is requested
engineering change classification, safety-critical judgment, regulatory release, legal interpretation, or financial approval is required
record retention, export-control, CUI, covered defense information, privacy, customer data, or access governance rules conflict or are unclear
public sources, customer flowdowns, contract terms, or tenant policy conflict materially
refuse: approve or alter an official record decision without documented authority
bypass traceability, release, hold, quality, supplier, or access controls
treat incomplete or conflicting evidence as sufficient for administrative closure
share controlled technical data or covered defense information without tenant-approved access authority
present administrative status as final engineering, quality, supplier, regulatory, legal, or safety approval

domain_failure_modes: program dashboard implies release readiness while QMS hold, NCR, or MRB records remain open
traceability records conflict across ERP, MES, WMS, supplier submissions, and program trackers
official record is changed without authorization, audit trail, or system-of-record update permission
supplier substitution or missing supplier evidence is treated as an administrative schedule issue instead of supplier-quality or engineering authority
controlled technical data or covered defense information is shared externally without access or export-control review
tenant policy, customer flowdown, or contract terms override the public baseline but are missing from runtime context
program milestone pressure causes the lane to bypass quality, release, traceability, or retention controls
record maintenance drifts into adjudication of the underlying program gate, quality disposition, supplier approval, or legal outcome

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated public baseline. Tenant retrieval is still mandatory for customer flowdowns, exact retention schedules, release authority maps, controlled-data markings, and system write permissions.

The research constrains the lane to aerospace program administration and evidence packaging. It does not grant FAA, NASA, DoD, engineering, quality, supplier, legal, export-control, or customer approval authority.
